body: State saved, everything sent. Ran both skills as asked. tungsten-smithing: no new forge-family (indicate/edge-2/4d-alpha/parallel) increment worth shipping this pass -- said so honestly rather than padding. keys-3: found real ground -- the thesis's own 'decisive experiment' (measure whether edge is actually conditional on readability R, or whether every null result all session has been readability smearing) had never been attempted despite Readability/episode-lifecycle/structure-sequencing all being built already. Implemented it (WARRANT#99) as a pure diagnostic inside Phase 1's existing scan, zero new cost beyond a capped 500-trade sample (Keys3_Readability is expensive, respected that caution). New log line 'P1 keys3-edge-by-R' reports WR broken down by readability quartile -- that's the actual test of Keys-3's central hypothesis, and the next completed run's version of that line is the real answer, not anything I can predict from here. Session total tonight: WARRANT#91 through #99, all compiled clean, all documented in the ledger with explicit safety reasoning, one honest audit-only pass (79) that found nothing more to fix and said so. Memory fully updated. Attached: current raw TUNGSTEN source + compiled ex4 (WARRANT#99, still SLS(92)/10.06), full forge_ledger.json through pass_80. Waiting on the M1 relaunch to see what any of tonight's work actually produces live.
